title: Rewrite .claude/settings.local.json hooks block to single-level Claude-adapter wiring
summary: Current schema has top-level `bash:` invoking COPILOT adapter AND nested `hooks[].command:` invoking CLAUDE adapter -- both fire per event. Replace 4 nested-event blocks with flat single-command entries pointing at hooks/claude/*.js only. UserPromptSubmit (line 28) and SessionStart (line 56) currently double-fire copilot+claude; PreCompact/Stop currently double-fire `true`+claude. Fix collapses each event to a single Claude command.
verification: After edit, `node -e 'JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync(".claude/settings.local.json","utf8"))'` succeeds. Manual smoke: spawn a fresh Claude Code session, observe SessionStart fires only the claude session-prime.js (verify by tracing dist/hooks/copilot/* not reached).

title: Build handlers/promotion-orchestrate.ts to wire rollback to advisor cache invalidation
summary: Zero production callers of rollbackPromotion or rollbackOnRegression confirmed by repo-wide grep. The promotion subsystem (6 modules under lib/promotion/) has no handler entry point. Build a new handler that orchestrates: gate evaluation -> shadow cycle -> two-cycle confirmation -> weight delta cap -> on-regression call rollbackOnRegression with invalidateCache: () => clearAdvisorBriefCache(). Register as MCP tool speckit_advisor_promotion_run in handlers/index.ts.
sampleInvocation: const trace = await rollbackOnRegression({ previousWeights, attemptedWeights, applyWeights: persistWeights, invalidateCache: clearAdvisorBriefCache, rollbackReason: 'gate-N regression' });
verification: Add tests/handlers/promotion-orchestrate.vitest.ts asserting: regression path calls invalidateCache exactly once; success path does not call invalidateCache; trace shape matches RollbackTrace.
supersedes: iter-9 F47 (binding gap identified, this provides the wire-up location)
reprioritizes: F37 #7 from P1 stale-cache-defect to P2 pre-emptive-correctness (rollback function is currently dead code outside tests)

title: Rename clearAdvisorBriefCacheForTests to clearAdvisorBriefCache + add deprecated re-export
summary: The function at lib/skill-advisor-brief.ts:384-387 is the misnamed production cache-clear seam. Its body is `advisorPromptCache.clear()` (production-safe, idempotent) but the 'ForTests' suffix discourages production use. Rename to clearAdvisorBriefCache; alias the old name as deprecated re-export to preserve test imports. This unblocks F52's handler from inlining `() => advisorPromptCache.clear()` or directly importing the singleton.
verification: Existing tests that import clearAdvisorBriefCacheForTests continue to compile; new handler in F52 imports the renamed symbol.
sources: lib/skill-advisor-brief.ts:384-387 (JSDoc literally lists 'tests AND session reset hooks' as use cases)
compoundsWith: F52 (atomic PR unit: build handler + rename seam)

title: F50 HMAC secret rotation does NOT cause test flakiness under default vitest config
summary: 5 tests perform twice-call cache-hit assertions: tests/handlers/advisor-recommend.vitest.ts:269-278 + :303-343, tests/legacy/advisor-prompt-cache.vitest.ts:13, tests/legacy/advisor-brief-producer.vitest.ts:255, tests/legacy/advisor-timing.vitest.ts:89,108. None call vi.resetModules() or vi.isolateModules(). DEFAULT_SECRET is computed at module load time; vitest default config preserves the module + secret across all it() blocks within a test file. F50 is a real production architectural invariant (per-process secret bounds stale-serve window) but is NOT a test-flakiness source.
sources: absent-grep vi.resetModules|vi.isolateModules in those test files
resolves: iter-9 F50 open question on test flakiness

title: rollbackOnRegression is a 1-line alias; F37 #7 fix at rollback.ts:48-51 covers both API surfaces
summary: lib/promotion/rollback.ts:75-87 -- rollbackOnRegression delegates to rollbackPromotion with no transformation (line 86: `return rollbackPromotion(args);`). F37 #7's surgical edit at rollback.ts:48-51 propagates automatically to the alias. F37 #7 footprint stays at ~5 lines, single function, no duplication needed.

title: Entire promotion subsystem (6 modules, 0 production handlers) is dead code outside tests
summary: Confirmed via repo-wide grep: lib/promotion/{gate-bundle,rollback,semantic-lock,shadow-cycle,two-cycle-requirement,weight-delta-cap}.ts have zero imports from handlers/ or any other production-facing surface. Only tests under tests/promotion/ import these modules. This is a much larger architectural gap than F52 alone -- the entire weight-promotion lifecycle has no production entry point. F52's handlers/promotion-orchestrate.ts is the seam, but the broader question of whether the promotion subsystem was ever production-wired in any prior phase deserves explicit decision-record treatment in implementation-summary.md.
recommendsForIter11: Audit the prior 014/013/012 phase implementation-summary.md files to determine whether the promotion subsystem was scoped as 'lib-only with handler in a future phase' or 'lib + handler in this phase, handler missed.' This determines whether F52 is a regression or an unfinished story.
